message:
fix(parser): escape glob special characters in archive paths

Previously, archive paths containing glob special characters like [, ],
or * would cause the glob pattern matching to fail or match unintended
files. For example, an archive path like "/data/test[1]/archive" would
be interpreted as a character class rather than a literal path.

This fix escapes the directory portion of the path using
glob::Pattern::escape() before appending the file matching pattern.
This ensures that only the intended pattern (e.g., "*.json") performs
glob matching while the base directory path is treated as a literal
string.

This is a critical bug fix as it affects any user whose archive paths
contain special characters, which is common in automatically generated
directory names or paths with version numbers like "project[v2]".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
